$metadata.description: Translation of primitives into business language. Encodes INTENT, not value. Readable by humans and AI agents.
$metadata.rule: Agents understand FUNCTION, not value. color.feedback.danger is an intent. red-700 is a decorative value.
$metadata._extensions-convention: The $extensions['com.agentica.usage'] fields are agent-ready metadata (DTCG W3C). Fields: role (semantic role), use[] (allowed usages), doNotUse (explicit contraindications), components[] (consuming components), decision (reference ADR). These fields are ignored by Style Dictionary at compile time — expected behavior. See ADR-052.

primary.$intent: Primary action — CTA, primary button. Accessible interactive teal: as text on the gray.1 #fcfcfc page background = 5.14:1; white text on top = 5.27:1 (WCAG AA). Distinct from the brand.primary identity teal (teal.9). See ADR-050 (corrects ADR-048).
primary.$description: Primary action color — CTA, primary buttons, interactive links. Accessible teal (5.14:1 on page background, 5.27:1 white text). PITFALL: brand.primary = teal.9 (brand identity) ≠ action.primary = teal.11 (accessible interactive).

com.agentica.usage.doNotUse: Decorative elements, surface backgrounds, disabled states. Never on a dark background without checking contrast. Do not confuse with brand.primary (teal.9), which is brand identity, not interactive action.

primary-hover.$intent: Primary action hover state — darkened (white text = 12:1). See ADR-048.
primary-hover.$description: Primary action hover/focus state — darkened teal (teal.12, white text 12:1). Exclusive use: visual feedback on hover or focus, never at rest.

com.agentica.usage.doNotUse: Resting state — never applied without user interaction. Never use as the static background of a button or link.

primary-disabled.$intent: Disabled primary action — inactive button background (WCAG 1.4.3 exempt)
primary-disabled.$description: Primary action background in the disabled state. Intentionally grayed out — WCAG 1.4.3 exempts inactive components. Never on a clickable element.

com.agentica.usage.doNotUse: Interactive button, resting state, neutral surface background. Never used on an element receiving user interactions.

danger.$description: System danger color — errors, destructive actions, critical alerts. Accessible red as text (high contrast). PITFALL: distinct from brand.accent (crimson.9), which is a vivid brand red, not an error state.

com.agentica.usage.doNotUse: Decorative elements, brand moments, non-semantic graphics. Never use for the primary action. THE TWO-REDS PITFALL: brand.accent (crimson.9) is a vivid red but = brand identity ≠ danger. Confusing them is a serious semantic error.

danger-subtle.$intent: Subtle background for the danger state

success.$description: System success color — confirmation, successful validation, positive state. Accessible green as text.

com.agentica.usage.doNotUse: Primary action, CTA button. Never replace action.primary with feedback.success for a 'positive' action — color semantics don't substitute for functional role.

success-subtle.$intent: Subtle background for the success state (ADR-045)

warning-subtle.$intent: Subtle background for the warning state (ADR-045)

info.$description: Neutral information color — contextual help, non-urgent informational statuses. Accessible blue as text. PITFALL: can appear interactive (blue) but triggers no action.

com.agentica.usage.doNotUse: Primary action — don't confuse informational blue (info) with action.primary (interactive teal). Info blue carries information, not an invitation to act.

info-text.$intent: Information text on light subtle background (badge info, banner info)

warning-text.$intent: Warning text on light subtle background (badge warning, banner warning)

page.$description: Main page background — the lowest-level surface in the elevation hierarchy (gray.1 = #fcfcfc). Never for a nested component surface.

com.agentica.usage.doNotUse: Component surface (card, modal, panel) — use background.surface. Never apply to a card nested inside another card (creates an unintended inversion of the elevation hierarchy).

inverse.$intent: Inverse dark surface — stats banner, footer (ADR-046)

inverse-raised.$intent: Raised dark surface on an inverse background — tooltips, popovers (ADR-046)

code.$intent: Code block background — dark surface dedicated to source code

code-raised.$intent: Raised background on the code surface — copy button at rest

code-raised-hover.$intent: Raised background on the code surface on hover — copy button hover

overlay-dark.$intent: Dark glassmorphism background — translucent card on marketing background (feature-card)
overlay-dark.$description: Semi-transparent dark background for frosted-glass cards (pair with backdrop-filter:blur()). A literal value not aliased to a primitive, like overlay.scrim/dark/medium/subtle — no existing primitive covers this hue + opacity. Token debt fixed (Figma audit of 2026-07-07): replaces a hardcoded value in agtc-feature-card.js.

com.agentica.usage.doNotUse: Background of an entire section — use background.inverse. Without backdrop-filter — the frosted effect is part of the intent.

scrim.$intent: Semi-transparent dark background — text container over an illustration (glassmorphism). The image stays 35% visible through the frosted glass.
scrim.$description: Used as the background of the .rd-cinematic-body and .hero-content-w containers in the cinematic pattern. Base: background.inverse (#0f1117) at 65% opacity. Pair with backdrop-filter:blur(10px) for the frosted-glass effect. 65% ratio: image visible, white text readable (WCAG AA guaranteed by the blur, which evens out the background).

com.agentica.usage.doNotUse: Background of an entire section — use background.inverse. Background with no backdrop-filter — the frosted effect is essential to the intent.

dark.$intent: Strong scrim — cinematic gradient on the text side (0% to 28% zone).
dark.$description: Initial value of the rd-bg-fade gradient. White text on this background reaches a ratio ≥ 7:1 (WCAG AAA).

medium.$intent: Medium scrim — transition zone of the cinematic gradient (50%).
medium.$description: Central value of the rd-bg-fade gradient — ensures visual continuity between the text zone and the illustration.

subtle.$intent: Light scrim — edge of the cinematic gradient (68%), lets the illustration show through.
subtle.$description: Final value of the rd-bg-fade gradient before the fully transparent zone.

primary.$description: High-readability primary text — body text, headings, important labels. Maximum contrast on the page background (gray.12 on gray.1 = 16:1).

com.agentica.usage.doNotUse: Text on a dark background (inverse surface) — insufficient contrast. On background.inverse, use text.on-dark. Never for disabled text — use text.disabled.

secondary.$description: Secondary text, labels, metadata — lighter than text.primary. WCAG AA on the page background. Never for critical content or error messages.

com.agentica.usage.doNotUse: Critical content (error messages, important data, page titles) — use text.primary. Never use for the main body of a form or an error alert.

disabled.$intent: Disabled text — 4.54:1 on white (WCAG 1.4.3 exempts inactive UI, kept accessible by convention)

on-dark.$intent: Strong text on an inverse surface — headings, hovered links (#fff, ADR-046)

on-dark-secondary.$intent: Secondary text on an inverse surface — links (10.8:1 on neutral.950, ADR-046)

on-dark-muted.$intent: Muted text on an inverse surface — AA floor (5.69:1 on neutral.950, ADR-046)

on-code.$intent: Text on the dark code surface — source code body

on-code-muted.$intent: Secondary text on the code surface — metadata (language, line number)

on-code-strong.$intent: Strong text on code surface — copy button (maximum contrast)

focus.$intent: Focus border — keyboard accessibility. Accessible interactive teal, unified with action.primary (5.14:1 on gray.1 #fcfcfc; non-text, ≥3:1 required). See ADR-050 (corrects ADR-048).
focus.$description: Keyboard focus ring — accessibility for mouse-free navigation. Unified with action.primary (5.14:1 on gray.1, ≥3:1 required by WCAG 1.4.11). Reserved exclusively for :focus-visible.

com.agentica.usage.doNotUse: Decorative border, separator, card or input border at rest. Never applied in CSS without the :focus-visible pseudo-selector — border.focus without :focus-visible is an accessibility violation.

on-dark.$intent: Separator on an inverse surface — non-text (ADR-046)

swatch.$intent: Outline of color swatches on a light background — non-text (ADR-046)

overlay-dark.$intent: Very subtle border — outlines a dark glassmorphism card (feature-card), non-text
overlay-dark.$description: Thin border (6% white opacity) to outline a translucent card on a dark marketing background, paired with background.overlay-dark. A literal value not aliased to a primitive — the white scale stops at white.12 (18% opacity), no step exists at 6%. Token debt fixed (Figma audit of 2026-07-07): replaces a hardcoded value in agtc-feature-card.js.

com.agentica.usage.doNotUse: Standard border on a light background — use border.default. Textual element — this border is non-text and decorative only.

strong.$intent: Emphasized non-text border — heavier than border.default, for decorative markers (e.g. changelog timeline dots) rather than standard control/card outlines

scale-bar.$intent: Decorative scale-visualization bar (spacing demo) — non-semantic, carries no information (ADR-046)

track-off.$intent: Toggle track in the inactive (off) state

ink.$intent: "Tactile Tech" illustration — dark masses, contrast anchor (ADR-051)

brand.$intent: Illustration — brand throughline, visual link to the product (ADR-051)

neutral.$intent: Illustration — masses secondaires, profondeur par superposition (ADR-051)

surface.$intent: Illustration — breathing room, light negative space (ADR-051)

primary.$intent: Primary brand color — identity, main CTA

primary-text.$intent: Brand text on light subtle background (badge brand, label) — 12:1 on white

primary-subtle.$intent: Subtle primary background — chips, tags, light badges

accent.$intent: Couleur d'accentuation — highlights, badges, CTA secondaire

accent-text.$intent: Accent text on light background — 7.1:1 WCAG AA+

secondary.$intent: Secondary brand color — solid dark background (12.2:1 with white)

secondary-text.$intent: Secondary text on light background — 13.8:1 WCAG AAA

tertiary.$intent: Tertiary brand color — completes the accent/secondary/tertiary decorative trio (e.g. brand-band shape fills)

logo-black.$intent: Black of the Agentica logo — monochrome version / light background. Reserved for brand identity elements (logo, wordmark).

use: brand icon on a light background
com.agentica.usage.doNotUse: Regular text (use text.primary), page background. Never for a generic UI component.

logo-gray.$intent: Gray of the Agentica logo (#6B7280) — muted version, brand baseline. Reserved for identity elements (faded logo, secondary pictogram).

com.agentica.usage.doNotUse: Secondary text (use text.secondary), borders. Never for a generic UI component.

header.$intent: Fixed header shadow — separation from scrolling content (ADR-046)

raised.$intent: Raised-element shadow — dropdown menus, mobile nav (ADR-046)

card-hover.$intent: Card hover shadow — brand-tinted lift (action.primary, ADR-046)

card.$intent: Resting card shadow — light elevation. Resolves the ADR-046 residual debt (consumed by component.card.elevated). See ADR-049.

_readme.$value: Three explicit groups (compact/normal/comfortable). compact uses floor(), comfortable uses ceil(), both divided by primitive.density.grid-unit (4) to guarantee 4px alignment. Technique: Sam's math equations in Tokens Studio. See ADR-025.

compact.$intent: 0.75 — scaling down, floor() for grid alignment

comfortable.$intent: 1.25 — scaling up, ceil() for grid alignment

padding-2xs.$intent: 2px — Sub-grid micro-spacing: badge.sm vertical padding (reserved for ultra-compact elements)

padding-xs.$intent: 4px — Espacement minimal : badge standard padding vertical

padding-sm.$intent: 8px — Espacement compact : badge.sm padding horizontal, cellules tableau compact

padding-md.$intent: 12px — Espacement standard composant : cellules tableau, badge standard padding horizontal, card compacte

padding-lg.$intent: 16px — Generous spacing: vertical padding for code blocks and banners

padding-xl.$intent: 20px — Large horizontal spacing: padding-x for code blocks and banners

padding-x.$intent: 16px — Horizontal spacing of controls (normal density). See space.compact or space.comfortable for other densities.

gap.$intent: 8px — Internal gap between elements of a control (normal density).

page-gutter.$intent: 80px — Horizontal gutter of documentation page sections (1440px layout). See ADR-052.

_readme.$value: Compact density — floor(value × 0.75 / 4) × 4. All values are multiples of 4px. Usage: dashboards, tables, data-dense tools.
Comfortable density — ceil(value × 1.25 / 4) × 4. All values are multiples of 4px. Usage: marketing, onboarding, reading pages.

control-tight.$intent: Focus-ring corner softening on inline/small elements — agtc-link text focus outline, agtc-input password-toggle focus outline. Not for general control fills/borders, see radius.control.

pill.$intent: Fully rounded radius — pill/circle shapes (badges, toggles, avatars). Raw value, no primitive alias: no primitive.radius step exists at this value, same precedent as component.badge.md.radius.

bold.$intent: Generic reusable bold weight, independent of any specific typography scale step (badges, labels, table headers, decorative emphasis) — distinct from the per-context typography.*.weight tokens, which stay for type-scale-specific use

display.$intent: Heaviest weight — hero/display-scale numerals and headings

fontFamily.$intent: System font — Atkinson Hyperlegible for maximum readability (low vision, WCAG)

family.$intent: Police monospace — code, tokens, valeurs techniques (ADR-028)

size.$intent: 12px — Emphasis on an annotation (e.g. a section eyebrow, badge) — same size as detail, stronger weight

weight.$intent: Bold — distinct from detail.weight (regular): reserved for small annotations that need emphasis (section labels, status badges)

size.$intent: 14px — Emphasis on a label (e.g. button text) — same size as label, stronger weight

weight.$intent: Bold — distinct from label.weight (medium): reserved for labels that need strong visual emphasis (CTA)

tighter.$intent: Very tight — large display numbers/digits (stats, KPIs) (ADR-068)

tight.$intent: Tight — h1 headings outside the hero (ADR-068)

snug.$intent: Slightly tight — logo, secondary stat numbers, home section headings (ADR-068)

heading.$intent: Subtle tightening — h2 headings, ADR page titles (ADR-068)

normal.$intent: Default — body text, labels, normal-case details

relaxed.$intent: Slightly wide — language buttons, contrast table headers (ADR-068)

wide.$intent: Wide — small-caps labels (e.g. code language indicator, eyebrow) where spacing compensates for the reduced readability of uppercase text

inline.$intent: Icon within text or a label

feature.$intent: 32px — Feature card icon, marketing section. Minimum required under data-context='marketing'. 4px grid: 8×4.

_readme.$value: Tokens reserved for the Narrative Marketing context (data-context=marketing). See .claude/rules/contexts-utilisation.md and ADR-057.

size.$intent: 60px — Marketing hook title: homepage hero, landing page. Never in a Product context.
12px — Short label above a hero title. Marketing only.

letter-spacing.$intent: Wide spacing — compensates for the reduced readability of small-caps text on the eyebrow label (ADR-067)

section-breathing.$intent: 96px — Generous breathing room between marketing sections. Contrasts with Product density (32px).

hero-gap.$intent: 120px — Vertical spacing of the marketing hero block. Never in a Product context.
